mt1-1-1.title: Hands-on: Recreate the discovery that started MT
mt1-1-1.intro: Recreate what Saxby, de Forest, and Doane uncovered: magnetize the steel plate with the yoke, dust on dry particles, and watch leakage fields pull them into crisp lines over the cracks. Rotate the yoke deliberately — direction is the whole trick — until all three cracks indicate.

tasks.label: Dust the plate and indicate crack A
tasks.hint: Energize the yoke, dust particles, then rotate until they gather into a sharp line.

tasks.label: Rotate the yoke to indicate crack B
tasks.hint: Leakage flux is strongest where the pole-to-pole flux crosses the crack near 90 degrees.

tasks.label: Find crack C to complete the set
tasks.hint: Crack C lies at a third orientation — keep sweeping the yoke around the plate.

mt1-1-2.title: Hands-on: Run your first complete magnetizing shots
mt1-1-2.intro: Walk the heart of the MT sequence you just outlined — magnetize, apply the particle bath, inspect, evaluate — twice on the same steel bar: once with a head shot and once with a coil shot. Finish with the kind of which-field-finds-which question your qualification exams will ask.

tasks.label: Coil shot: energize, bathe, reveal the transverse crack
tasks.hint: The encircling coil drives flux along the bar, so it crosses cracks running around the bar.

tasks.label: Head shot: reveal the longitudinal crack
tasks.hint: Current through the bar makes circular flux, which crosses cracks running along its length.

tasks.label: Pass the check: which field finds which crack?
tasks.hint: Flux must cross a crack to reveal it — match each field to the crack it cuts across.

mt1-1-3.title: Hands-on: Prove that direction decides detection
mt1-1-3.intro: Every crack on this plate is real, yet at the starting yoke angle none of them indicates — the flux runs too close to parallel to each one. Rotate the yoke to make each crack appear and prove the limitation yourself: absence of an indication is not absence of a discontinuity, so full coverage demands two roughly perpendicular magnetizations.

tasks.label: Rotate the yoke until silent crack A indicates
tasks.hint: At the start the flux is only 45 degrees off crack A — swing the yoke until it crosses A nearly square.

tasks.label: Find the second window: indicate crack B
tasks.hint: B's normal sits 45 degrees beyond A's — keep rotating past the angle where A appeared.

tasks.label: Indicate crack C and watch A stay dark
tasks.hint: C only shows when flux runs nearly parallel to A — no single shot can cover both, hence two magnetizations.
